description: NIST AI RMF function and subcategory mapping to AumOS governance protocols
source: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) AI 100-1

functions.title: Govern
functions.description: Cultivate and implement a culture of risk management within organizations designing, developing, deploying, or using AI systems.

subcategories.title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices
subcategories.description: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.

subcategories.title: Accountability structures
subcategories.description: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for AI risk management.

subcategories.title: Workforce diversity and AI risk management
subcategories.description: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks.

subcategories.title: Organizational risk tolerances
subcategories.description: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

subcategories.title: Processes for ongoing monitoring
subcategories.description: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

subcategories.title: Policies and procedures for third-party AI
subcategories.description: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

functions.title: Map
functions.description: Context is established and understood so that the risks related to AI systems can be identified.

subcategories.title: Context is established
subcategories.description: Context is established and understood, including the intended purpose, potential positive and negative impacts, and possible benefits of the AI system.

subcategories.title: Categorization of AI systems
subcategories.description: Categorization of the AI system is performed.

subcategories.title: AI capabilities and limitations
subcategories.description: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.

subcategories.title: Risks and impacts
subcategories.description: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system, including third-party software and data.

subcategories.title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, and society
subcategories.description: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact based on expected use, past uses of similar AI systems, and other contextual factors are identified and documented.

functions.title: Measure
functions.description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked using quantitative, qualitative, or mixed-method tools, techniques, and methodologies.

subcategories.title: Appropriate methods and metrics
subcategories.description: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.

subcategories.title: AI systems evaluated for trustworthiness
subcategories.description: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.

subcategories.title: Mechanisms for tracking AI risks over time
subcategories.description: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.

subcategories.title: Feedback from affected communities
subcategories.description: Feedback about efficacy of measurement is collected and assessed.

functions.title: Manage
functions.description: Risks are prioritized and acted upon based on a projected impact.

subcategories.title: AI risks are prioritized and responded to
subcategories.description: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.

subcategories.title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits
subcategories.description: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.

subcategories.title: AI risks and benefits from third parties
subcategories.description: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.

subcategories.title: Risk treatments including response and recovery
subcategories.description: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented.
